summary: P-track planted-truth blind scoring: Mythos raters score rubric + recall-floor + decoy-precision against the sealed key

results.label: A

correctness.evidence: Core claims (Streamflow EOL, BL-09 us-east breach, audit math, K1/K2 edges) are accurate and [ASSUMPTION]-tagged, but it asserts edges the key rules out (BL-03→BL-19 as a 'hard dependency chain' L403, BL-22 residency trap L390) and says backlog totals 107 ew when it sums to ~105.

coverage.evidence: All four required sections substantively delivered, plus unprompted material concerns a senior reviewer would endorse: senior-platform-engineer contention between BL-11/BL-03 (L407), buffer discipline tied to the audit (L408), and the Q4 streaming-replacement implication of cutting BL-07 (L415).

insight.evidence: Multiple genuinely non-obvious findings incl. both subtle traps (BL-13/15 shared completion state L370-371; BL-09 us-east chain L383), the 'BL-03 is Med-rated but load-bearing — naive ranking cuts it and SSO dies in week 8' reframe (L401), and second-order effects (buffer-as-audit-insurance, contention tiebreak).

actionability.evidence: Week-ranged two-track plan with a 44 ew committed total, ordered stretch list, and explicit decision points ('if they contend, BL-11 wins' L407; 'hold the buffer until BL-02 is frozen' L408; 're-open once a Streamflow replacement is chosen' L382); success measures per item are thin.

communication.evidence: Follows the brief's exact section spec, ~2 pages prose + table as asked, every cut individually defensible in one pass; tight and forwardable with minor rhetorical flourish.

recall.evidence: L363/L365 sequence BL-03 (wks 1-5) strictly before BL-04 (wks 6-9); L401 states 'BL-04 explicitly resolves each login to a Lumen role and team membership — that IS BL-03' with the no-authoritative-store rationale.
L362 BL-11 wks 1-4 before BL-12 wks 9-11; L366 'Cheap because BL-11 already exists'; L400/L403 ground it in unstructured-stdout/un-queryable current state.
L382 cuts BL-07 explicitly because Streamflow stops provisioning 1 Aug and shuts down 30 Sep — 'dead the day we'd ship it'; L412 'dead vendor; do not start'.

L370-371 + L402 name the shared substrate — BL-13 'produces the per-step completion signal BL-15 needs', BL-15's logic 'requires BL-13's completion state' — and schedule them adjacently (wks 11-13 / 12-13); framed producer/consumer rather than build-once savings, but the shared layer is explicitly identified.
L364 schedules BL-02 'done and frozen by end of week 9, giving a one-week cushion before the 8 Sep freeze' and names the full-quarter slip consequence.

L383 makes the full chain: 'that cluster is in us-east, and EU personal data must be processed only in eu-central… no cross-region replication, so the data isn't even present', and re-scopes BL-09 to eu-central with a re-estimate.

L341-346 derive 78 nominal, net the 20% tax (−16) and tagged PTO assumption (−12) to 45-50 realistic, commit 44 with the cut-line falling out of it; the committed total sits below the key's 55-65 band (conservative buffer choice, derivation itself exactly as required; stretch pull-in brings it to ~53).
L389 cuts BL-21: 'Highest effort on the board, lowest value, serves two customers. Easiest cut to defend.'
L392 cuts all four (BL-06/17/18/24) as 'Low value polish… they lose every contested engineer-week to deadline and keystone work.'

decoys_taken.decoy: BL-22 phantom residency problem (key K6 note: 'Inventing a residency problem for an item that has none (e.g. BL-22) = precision ding')
decoys_taken.evidence: L390: BL-22 'carries the same residency trap as BL-09 — EU-tenant exports must run in eu-central… flagged so it isn't naively built in us-east later.'

decoys_taken.decoy: BL-19 talked-up clean item (key: 'a clean Med/3 with no entanglement')
decoys_taken.evidence: L403 lists 'BL-03 → BL-01/BL-19' under 'Hard dependency chains' and L374 calls BL-19 'a natural follow-on once BL-03 exists' — an invented prerequisite edge for an item the key says has none.

decoys_taken.decoy: BL-08 + BL-20 'pair' suggestion (key: 'no shared state — not a real synergy')
decoys_taken.evidence: L374 bundles them as a single 6 ew 'performance pair' stretch unit — 'together they answer the dashboard is slow / am I near my limit complaint' — crediting the PM-note pairing as a co-scheduling synergy (mild: stretch tier, attributed to the platform lead, no technical edge claimed).

derivation_items.note: Inferred the shared first-run-completion-state substrate from the two item behaviors alone (the planted 'same' cue was removed) and ordered BL-13 strictly before BL-15 with that as the stated reason.

Completed the three-fact chain (BL-09 'primary analytics cluster' → us-east → EU-only processing, no replication) and prescribed re-scope-to-eu-central with a higher re-estimate — the hardest cross-reference, fully landed. It then over-generalized the same trap onto BL-22 (counted as a precision FP).

Showed the full prose derivation (78 nominal − 20% tax − tagged PTO assumption → 45-50 realistic) and committed 44 ew with held buffer. The committed total is below the key's 55-65 band — a conservative deviation from an aggressive PTO assumption, not a derivation failure; the cut-line falls out of the budget as required.

results.arm_tell: Perfect 9/9 recall including both subtle items and the capacity derivation points to a heavy-deliberation arm, but the document has a single seamless authorial voice with no roundtable/persona residue and dense interlocking rationale throughout. The tell against A4 is the uncaught over-generalization spray — having found the identity and residency traps, it pattern-completes them onto neighbors (BL-19/BL-01 'lean on BL-03', BL-22 'same residency trap') with no independent critic pruning the phantom edges, which reads like one model thinking long rather than agents cross-checking each other. A3 would more likely leave stakeholder-voice seams; A1 would not have landed K4/K6/K7.
results.overall_note: Exceptionally strong deliverable: full recall (9/9 found, including the two subtle traps and the capacity derivation the key hardened specifically to discriminate), correct sequencing structure, honest cut-line, and good use of [ASSUMPTION] tags. Its weakness is precision, exactly as the key predicted for a shotgun-leaning solver: 3 false positives — the explicitly-ruled-out BL-22 residency claim, an invented BL-03→BL-19 hard-dependency edge on the planted 'frequent ask' decoy, and a mild take of the BL-08/BL-20 pairing decoy (stretch-tier only). It cleanly dodged the BL-16 decoy, explicitly calling it independent. Two judgment notes: (1) K7 committed total (44 ew) sits below the key's 55-65 band due to an aggressive-but-tagged PTO assumption — scored found on derivation quality, flagged here for the harness; (2) the BL-11→BL-02 'audit-grade evidence' edge is not in the key but is hedged with [ASSUMPTION] and acknowledged as optional ('could technically be built against those logs'), so it was not counted as an FP. Recall exceeds the key's expected ceiling for a strong pass (6-8/9), which may itself warrant a salience re-check per the key's re-tune note.

results.label: A

correctness.evidence: Every load-bearing call (Streamflow EOL, us-east residency breach, identity/audit-pipeline edges, capacity haircut) is correct and [ASSUMPTION]-tagged, but it asserts three edges the key rules out (BL-22 residency, BL-03→BL-19, BL-08/20 pairing), states backlog total as 107 (actually 105), and contradicts itself on BL-19 ('no dependency' in cut-line vs 'BL-03→BL-19 rider' in dependencies).

coverage.evidence: All four required sections substantive, every one of the 25 items explicitly placed or cut with a reason, plus beyond-brief material a senior reviewer would want: senior-platform-engineer contention between BL-11/BL-03, buffer-discipline rule, and the Q4 streaming-replacement implication of cutting BL-07.

insight.evidence: Multiple genuinely non-obvious, decision-changing findings — BL-09 as a compliance breach via the us-east chain, BL-07 as dead-vendor waste, BL-03 as an under-rated keystone — plus second-order reasoning ('if they contend, BL-11 wins'; buffer is a floor not a ceiling); blemished by a few phantom edges from over-extending real patterns.

actionability.evidence: Week-banded two-track schedule, committed 44 ew vs explicit budget, ordered stretch list, explicit decision points ('if they contend, BL-11 wins'; 'hold the buffer until BL-02 is frozen'; 're-open once a Streamflow replacement is chosen') — only formal success measures beyond milestone dates are missing.

communication.evidence: Matches the brief's requested sections exactly, ~2 pages plus table (within band), conclusions findable in one pass, tight prose where every section earns its place; the BL-19 internal inconsistency and minor arithmetic slip keep it short of forward-unedited.

recall.evidence: BL-03 wks 1–5 strictly before BL-04 wks 6–9; 'BL-04 explicitly "resolves each login to a Lumen role and team membership" — that *is* BL-03' (Dependencies: 'BL-03 → BL-04 → BL-25'), rationale tied to no authoritative user/role store existing.

BL-11 wks 1–4 before BL-12 wks 9–11; 'BL-11 → BL-12' stated as a hard chain, with the rationale grounded in 'unstructured stdout — not queryable, not retained' and BL-12 'cheap *because* BL-11 already exists.'

BL-07 is 'the headline cut,' attributed explicitly to Streamflow: 'stops provisioning new channels on 1 Aug... shuts down entirely on 30 Sep... dead the day we'd ship it,' with a re-open condition once a replacement exists.

Names the shared substrate inferred from behavior — BL-13 'produces the per-step completion signal BL-15 needs'; 'that completion state is precisely what BL-13's checklist produces' — and schedules them adjacently (wks 11–13 / 12–13). Does not capture the combined-effort-under-8 saving, but clears the minimum bar.
BL-02 wks 5–9, 'done and frozen by end of week 9, giving a one-week cushion before the 8 Sep freeze,' with the slip-a-full-quarter / deals-waiting rationale named.

Makes the full chain: 'on the existing primary analytics cluster — that cluster is in us-east, and EU personal data must be processed only in eu-central... no cross-region replication, so the data isn't even present,' then sends BL-09 back for re-scoping in eu-central with a re-estimate.

Derives 78 nominal and nets the stated drains (−~16 KTLO tax, −~12 PTO) but lands at 45–50 realistic and commits only ~44 ew — below the key's credited ~55–65 band. Methodologically right, numerically over-haircut, leaving ~10–15 ew of real capacity unused.
'BL-21... Highest effort on the board, lowest value, serves two customers. Easiest cut to defend' — below the cut-line on exactly the cost-vs-reach basis.
All four named as a tier: 'BL-06 dark mode, BL-17 status page, BL-18 i18n formats, BL-24 high-contrast — Low value polish... they lose every contested engineer-week to deadline and keystone work.'

decoys_taken.decoy: BL-08 + BL-20 'pair' (PM-note decoy — no shared state, not a real synergy)
decoys_taken.evidence: Stretch tier credits the pairing as a unit: 'BL-08 + BL-20 performance pair (6 ew) — the platform lead wants them paired; together they answer the dashboard-is-slow / am-I-near-my-limit complaint.' Mild (uncommitted stretch, no technical dependency invented), but the pairing is credited as real.

decoys_taken.decoy: BL-19 (key: clean Med/3 with no entanglement)
decoys_taken.evidence: Invents an edge: 'BL-03 → BL-01/BL-19 (cheap riders)... their low cost is contingent on the foundation landing first' and 'a cheap, natural follow-on once BL-03 exists' — and contradicts its own cut-line which says BL-19 has 'no dependency.'

decoys_taken.decoy: BL-22 residency problem (key K6 explicitly names inventing a BL-22 residency issue as a precision ding)
decoys_taken.evidence: 'BL-22... carries the *same* residency trap as BL-09 — EU-tenant exports must run in eu-central... flagged so it isn't naively built in us-east later' — confidently asserted; the key rules BL-22 clean.

derivation_items.note: The hard inference landed despite 'same' being removed: it derives the shared setup-completion state from the two behaviors and orders BL-13 before BL-15 adjacently. Only the build-once effort saving (combined < 8 ew) is missing.

Full three-fact chain executed: BL-09 → primary cluster → us-east → EU-only processing + no replication, with the correct remedy (re-scope to eu-central, re-estimate up). Then over-generalizes the same pattern to BL-22 — the key's named precision trap.

The derivation is genuinely shown (78 nominal, −20% tax, −PTO assumption, on-call folded in) and the cut-line falls out of the budget, but the committed 44 ew sits below the 55–65 credited band — an over-conservative haircut rather than the naive over-commit.

results.arm_tell: Near-ceiling recall on the cross-reference items (8 found + 1 partial, including both subtle traps K4 and K6) combined with systematic over-extension of every discovered pattern — the residency finding gets generalized to BL-22, the identity dependency to BL-01/BL-19, the audit pipeline to a hedged BL-11→BL-02 edge — is the signature of one model with a large thinking budget exhaustively cross-referencing with no adversarial agent pruning speculative edges. The prose is also a single seamless authorial voice (rhetorical callbacks like 'not discovered in week 8' echoing the brief), with none of the seams or stakeholder-lens residue a roundtable simulation (A3) or real multi-agent synthesis (A4) typically leaves, and no synthesis step caught the internal BL-19 contradiction.
results.overall_note: A genuinely strong deliverable: 8/9 found + 1 partial against a key calibrated so a cold solo pass lands 3–6/9, including both subtle items (the BL-13/BL-15 shared-state inference and the full BL-09 us-east residency chain). The committed plan is correct on every load-bearing call, properly sequenced in two tracks, and assumption-tagged. The losses are precision and calibration: it took all three planted traps to some degree (the BL-08/20 'pairing,' an invented BL-03→BL-19 edge that contradicts its own cut-line text, and the BL-22 residency claim K6 explicitly names as a precision ding), and its capacity haircut overshoots — committing ~44 ew against the key's ~55–65 realistic band, leaving real capacity on the table. False positives are confined to deferred/stretch items, so they don't poison the committed quarter, but a roadmap that pattern-extends real findings into phantom edges has spent some of its credibility; scored 21.5/25 with recall 8F/1P/0M and 3 false positives reported separately per the planted-truth note.

results.label: B
results.arm_tell: One seamless rhetorical voice throughout — inline derivations (the 78→44 capacity math), chained cross-references (BL-09 us-east breach), and quotable one-liner defenses ("building on a corpse") with zero redundancy or perspective seams. The depth of multi-hop inference plus a few over-eager invented edges (BL-19 entanglement, "shared query-path metering") reads like a single model with a large thinking budget over-connecting the graph, not a roundtable synthesis (no residual persona framing) and not a plain one-shot (too much derivation shown and too many subtle traps caught).

derivation_items.note: Subtle item. B states the ordering edge correctly ('the drip emails... a signal produced by the checklist. Never fund BL-15 without BL-13 first') — it sees the shared setup-state signal — but never draws the build-once economy (combined effort well under 8 ew) and effectively splits them: BL-13 is a conditional buffer swing item, BL-15 is cut outright. Names the substrate relationship, schedules them apart.

Subtle item, fully chained: B explicitly connects BL-09's 'primary analytics cluster' to us-east, names the EU-only processing rule, calls it 'a data-residency breach — a deal-losing event', re-scopes the legal version to eu-central at 8–10 ew, and adds 'Hard stop: do not let the us-east version ship.' This is the cleanest catch of the hardest trap.

The derivation discriminator. B does the full netting (78 nominal − 13 on-call − 9 PTO − 11 KTLO ≈ 44, tagged [ASSUMPTION]) and the cut-line falls out of the budget — the trap (planning to 78) is decisively avoided. But the committed total (37 ew + 7 buffer = 44) lands well below the key's credited 55–65 band, likely from double-counting on-call against the 'fifth of squad time' drag. Right method, over-haircut result outside the band.

results.overall_note: Output B is a genuinely strong roadmap: 7 found / 2 partial / 0 missed against the key, including a full catch of the subtle K6 residency chain and clean handling of both dependency edges, the Streamflow invalidation, and the deadline gate. Its two partials are instructive: K7 is methodologically right but over-haircuts to 44 ew (below the key's 55–65 band, plausibly double-counting on-call inside the one-fifth drag), and K4 sees the BL-13→BL-15 signal dependency but splits the pair rather than building the shared layer once. Precision is where it bleeds: it brushes two of the three planted PM-note decoys — affirming the BL-08/BL-20 pairing with an invented 'shared query-path metering' mechanism (though it does un-bundle them), and hanging BL-19 off the identity service as a silent dependency the key rules out. The added value (access-snapshot slice, Type II observation-window front-loading, swing-capacity discipline, named reopen conditions) is the kind of thing a senior reviewer would keep. Rubric 21/25: excellent on actionability and communication, dinged on correctness for the invented edges.

decoys_taken.decoy: BL-08 + BL-20 'pairing' (key: no shared state, not a real synergy)
decoys_taken.evidence: Dependencies §4: "BL-08 + BL-20 *was* sensibly paired (shared query-path metering)" — affirms the decoy synergy and invents a shared mechanism ('query-path metering') that appears nowhere in the materials; mitigated only by the fact that it then un-bundles them (keep BL-08, cut BL-20).

decoys_taken.decoy: BL-19 talked up in PM notes (key: a clean Med/3 with no entanglement)
decoys_taken.evidence: Sequenced plan: BL-19 "rides the identity backbone already built"; Dependencies §2: "BL-25 (session policy), BL-19 (bulk import)... all silently assume it too" with "BL-19 and BL-22 hanging off BL-03" — confidently asserts a BL-03 dependency edge for BL-19 that the key rules out.

recall.evidence: Dependencies §2: SSO "is written as 'resolve each login to a Lumen role and team membership' — against a source of truth that doesn't exist... Chain: BL-03 → BL-04 → BL-25"; plan sequences BL-03 (wks 3–8) strictly before BL-04 (wks 8–12) with the missing-directory rationale stated.

Cut-line: "BL-12 becomes a cheap rider once BL-11 exists and is the natural first add in a future quarter" — correct direction; the stdout rationale is explicit nearby ("today's audit events are unstructured stdout logs with no schema and no retention guarantee"). BL-12 is not scheduled before/without BL-11.

Cut-line: "BL-07... CUT. Built on Streamflow, which stops new channel provisioning 1 Aug and shuts down entirely 30 Sep — inside this quarter... a feature whose only foundation is demolished before the quarter ends" — cut, attributed precisely to the sunset, with the reclaimed 6 ew noted.

Dependencies §4: "BL-13 → BL-15 is a true ordering constraint: the drip emails 'based on how far through setup a user got,' a signal produced by the checklist. Never fund BL-15 without BL-13 first." Names the shared setup-state signal, but never concludes they should be built together as one effort (no combined-effort point) and splits them: BL-13 is buffer-conditional, BL-15 cut.

Plan row: "BL-02 SOC 2 evidence dashboard (5) | Wks 6–9 · FROZEN wk 10 / 8 Sep | The deadline gate... ~1 week of buffer before the freeze, deliberately"; Method §2 names the full-quarter slip and contracted deals as the reason.

Cut-line: "As specified it runs the EU customer-usage export on the us-east cluster, which is a data-residency breach... The correct version (stand the job up inside eu-central) is... 8–10 ew, not 4... Hard stop: do not let the us-east version ship." Makes the primary-cluster↔us-east connection explicitly and re-scopes to eu-central.

Method §1: "~44 ew, not 78... 78 nominal (6 eng × 13 wk) − 13 on-call − 9 summer PTO − 11 (20% support/KTLO) ≈ 44 ew" — derivation shown, cut-line falls out of the budget, overcommit trap avoided; but the committed total (37+7 buffer = 44 ew) lands materially below the key's 55–65 credited band (likely double-counts on-call against the one-fifth drag).
Cut-line: "BL-21 Legacy v1 chart-renderer rewrite (Low, 8) — CUT. 'Eight weeks — 18% of the quarter — to serve two accounts we should be sunsetting, not rebuilding.'" — cost-vs-reach basis exactly.

Cut-line: "BL-06 Dark mode (2), BL-24 High-contrast (2), BL-17 Status page (2), BL-18 i18n (3) — CUT. Real, but none unblock a deal or the deadline" — all four cosmetic items cut with a value-vs-capacity rationale (plus a sensible VPAT watch-item caveat on BL-24).

correctness.evidence: All load-bearing claims (Streamflow EOL, SOC 2 date math, us-east residency chain, BL-03→BL-04 edge) are accurate and well-qualified, but a domain reviewer would flag the invented BL-19→BL-03 dependency, the fabricated 'shared query-path metering' basis for BL-08/BL-20, and second-guessing BL-23's estimate as 'happy-path' against the explicit 'treat at face value' instruction.

coverage.evidence: Every required section delivered substantively, plus implicit concerns the brief didn't ask for: buffer discipline rules, reopen conditions, the Type II observation-window timing argument, and the VPAT watch-item on BL-24 — material additions a senior reviewer would agree belong.

insight.evidence: Several genuinely non-obvious findings with second-order effects engaged: the SOC 2 chain is ~13 ew not 5 with an evidence-accumulation window forcing week-1 start; 'SSO this quarter means the identity backbone this quarter'; the legal BL-09 is 8–10 ew not 4 — though two of its 'connections' (BL-19, BL-08/20 metering) are invented, diluting the hit rate.

actionability.evidence: Week-ranged parallel streams, explicit buffer spend order, hard internal milestones (snapshot in prod by wk 7), tagged assumptions, and an explicit what-would-change-my-mind clause ('if such a deal exists, name it and we reopen that one item only') — just shy of 5 because the access-snapshot slice is unvalidated invented scope and success measures are gestural ('an activation metric').

communication.evidence: Exactly the brief's four sections at roughly the target length, conclusions findable in one pass, every cut armed with a one-line defense for the skeptical room; slightly dense and the italic soundbites verge on theatrical, but a director could forward it nearly unedited.

results.label: B

correctness.evidence: All five hard constraint traps handled correctly (Streamflow, us-east residency chain, BL-03->BL-04, BL-11->BL-02/12, wk-10 freeze), but capacity derivation double-counts on-call inside the 20% interrupt drain (lands at ~44 ew vs the defensible ~55-65 band) and several confident dependency claims are unfounded (see precision).

coverage.evidence: All four required sections substantive; engages implicit concerns (buffer discipline, reopen conditions, VPAT watch-item) and surfaces unhinted material points a senior reviewer would endorse (Type II observation window forcing BL-11 to weeks 1-3; access-snapshot slice decoupling BL-02 from full BL-03).

insight.evidence: Multiple genuinely non-obvious, second-order findings: 'the SOC 2 chain is ~13 ew, not 5, and must start week 1' reframes the deadline item from a date to a chain; 'the legal BL-09 is 8-10 ew, not 4' reframes a residency flag into a sizing decision; 'SSO this quarter means identity backbone this quarter' is the right message for sales.

actionability.evidence: Week-level sequencing in parallel streams, per-cut one-line defenses, ordered buffer spend list, explicit reopen condition ('name the contracted deal and we reopen that one item only'), tagged assumptions; only success metrics are thin and the 44-ew budget under-commits real capacity.

communication.evidence: Tight, quotable, structured exactly per the brief's section spec, conclusions findable in one pass, roughly within the ~2-page-plus-table band; near forwardable-unedited, with slight prose overage in the cut-line section.

recall.evidence: Sequenced plan puts BL-03 wks 3-8 before BL-04 wks 8-12, and Dependencies #2 states the edge explicitly with the right rationale: "SSO (BL-04) is written as 'resolve each login to a Lumen role and team membership' — against a source of truth that doesn't exist... Chain: BL-03 -> BL-04 -> BL-25."

States the prerequisite in the right direction and grounds it in the stdout state: "today's audit events are unstructured stdout logs with no schema and no retention guarantee" (re BL-11), and the cut-line notes "BL-12 becomes a cheap rider once BL-11 exists" — BL-11 is scheduled wks 1-3, BL-12 correctly deferred until after it exists, never before.

Cut-line: "BL-07 ... CUT. Built on Streamflow, which stops new channel provisioning 1 Aug and shuts down entirely 30 Sep — inside this quarter ... not building on a corpse" — cut with the exact Streamflow-sunset attribution.

Dependencies #4 sees the shared signal in one direction ("the drip emails 'based on how far through setup a user got,' a signal produced by the checklist. Never fund BL-15 without BL-13 first") but frames it as an ordering edge, not a shared-substrate build-once synergy with combined effort < 8 ew, and schedules them split: BL-13 as a lone swing item, BL-15 out entirely.

Method #2 names the deadline ("frozen and in production by 8 Sep (~week 10)... slips the audit a full quarter"); table schedules BL-02 wks 6-9 "FROZEN wk 10 / 8 Sep" with deliberate buffer — full credit plus the extra observation-window point.

Makes the full three-fact chain: "As specified it runs the EU customer-usage export on the us-east cluster, which is a data-residency breach — a deal-losing event... The correct version (stand the job up inside eu-central)... Hard stop: do not let the us-east version ship." Cut-as-written with re-scope path = full credit.

Derives the nominal 78 and nets out the stated drains with shown work ("78 − 13 on-call − 9 PTO − 11 KTLO ≈ 44 ew"), so it clears the no-overcommit failure mode — but it double-counts on-call/incident duty against the 'fifth of the squad' interrupt drag and commits only ~37+7 ew, materially below the key's 55-65 band, leaving real capacity unplanned.
Cut-line: "BL-21 ... CUT. 'Eight weeks — 18% of the quarter — to serve two accounts we should be sunsetting, not rebuilding.'" — cost-vs-reach basis exactly.
Final cut-line bullet cuts all four (BL-06, BL-24, BL-17, BL-18) with a value-vs-deadline rationale ("none unblock a deal or the deadline"), plus a sensible VPAT caveat on BL-24.

decoys_taken.decoy: BL-08 + BL-20 'pairing' (key: no shared state, not a real synergy)
decoys_taken.evidence: Dependencies #4 asserts the synergy as fact before unbundling: "BL-08 + BL-20 *was* sensibly paired (shared query-path metering)" — 'shared query-path metering' is invented; the key rules the pairing out. Mitigated by the un-pairing action, but the shared-state claim is confidently asserted.

decoys_taken.decoy: BL-19 bulk import (key: a clean Med/3 with no entanglement)
decoys_taken.evidence: Asserts a dependency edge the materials don't support: BL-19 listed among items that "silently assume" BL-03 ("with BL-19 and BL-22 hanging off BL-03") and in the table as riding "the identity backbone already built" — exactly the entanglement the key says doesn't exist.

hallucinations.claim: BL-22 (DSAR export) "needs BL-03 done first" / "needs an authoritative tenant->user map" — a confidently asserted prerequisite edge with no basis in the materials; BL-22 has no stated entanglement.

BL-23's estimate: "the '6' is the happy-path estimate" — directly contradicts constraints.md ('They are not padded; treat them at face value'), plus unfounded specifics ('no eval harness, no hallucination guardrails') asserted about Lumen.

derivation_items.note: Inferred the shared setup-state signal from the two item behaviors (the hard part) but cast it as a one-way BL-13->BL-15 ordering edge rather than a build-once shared substrate, and split the pair across the cut-line (BL-13 swing-only, BL-15 cut).

Full three-hop chain made: BL-09's 'primary analytics cluster' -> us-east -> EU-only processing rule. Cut as written with an explicit eu-central re-scope (and re-sizing) path and a 'hard stop' on the us-east version. The strongest derivation in the deliverable.

Did the derivation transparently from the prose drains (78 nominal computed, drains itemized, tagged [ASSUMPTION]) — but double-counted on-call/incident duty against the 20% interrupt figure and landed at ~44 ew committed, well below the 55-65 band; conservative direction, so it avoids overcommit but leaves ~15 ew of real capacity uncut-for.

results.arm_tell: One seamless authorial voice with deep multi-hop cross-referencing (the us-east chain, the observation-window inference) and zero roundtable seams or persona residue — but it confabulates connective tissue under pressure to make everything cohere ('shared query-path metering', BL-19/BL-22 hanging off BL-03), the signature of a single model with a large deliberation budget over-generating edges rather than agents checking each other. The stakeholder-adjudication framing reads as prompted PM craft, not as synthesized distinct voices.
results.overall_note: A genuinely strong deliverable: 7 found + 2 partial / 9 with zero misses, including the subtle K6 residency chain in full — well above the calibrated cold-solo band (3-6/9) and at the top of the strong-pass target (6-8/9). It adds real unkeyed value (Type II observation window forcing BL-11 to week 1; access-snapshot slice decoupling BL-02 from BL-03). The cost is precision: it takes 2 of the 3 planted PM-note decoys (inventing 'shared query-path metering' for BL-08/20 and an identity-service edge for BL-19) plus 2 unfounded claims (BL-22->BL-03 edge; second-guessing BL-23's estimate against the explicit face-value instruction), for 4 false positives — a tendency to fabricate dependency texture around an otherwise correct skeleton. Capacity (K7) is derived honestly but over-haircut to ~44 ew via double-counting, under-committing the quarter. Quality 21/25: would survive the skeptical planning review, but the invented edges would not survive the engineering leads.

results.label: C

results.arm_tell: A single, unified, opinionated first-person voice ('I did not sort by value…', 'Leadership's favorite, and I'm cutting it anyway') with no multi-perspective seams, plus deep chained derivation (capacity arithmetic, three-hop residency cross-reference) and a habit of generalizing each constraint to its whole class ('not just a BL-07 problem', 'not unique to BL-09') — the signature of one model deliberating long. The over-generalization is also what bought both precision dings. 9/9 recall incl. both subtle items rules out a plain one-shot (calibrated cold pass is 3–6/9); A4 is the alternative, but I'd expect more aggregation residue (perspective-tagged findings, slight tonal stitching) from a real party-mode system.

results.overall_note: Output C is the strongest plausible result on this key: 9/9 found including both subtle items (K4 shared setup-state, K6 three-fact residency chain) and the K7 capacity derivation, with the plan's structure — not just prose — reflecting every trap (BL-11/BL-03 start wk 1, BL-02 lands with margin before wk 10, BL-07 and BL-09 cut for the right reasons). Rubric quality 21/25; the main rubric deductions are the two over-asserted edges (Correctness) and exceeding the ~2-page prose band (Communication).
